name: OS Package Installer
description: Install packages from the operating system's package manager.

dir.description:
Path to directory containing ecosystem-specific package files.
Path to a directory containing files named '{prefix}-{type}', where
'{prefix}' is one of: apt, apk, dnf, zypper, pacman — corresponding to the
detected package ecosystem — and '{type}' is one of:
- prescript: executed before repository addition (e.g. GPG key import)
- repo: repository definition, written verbatim as a drop-in config file
- pkg: newline-separated package specifications to install
- script: executed after package installation (e.g. post-install setup)
All files are optional. If none are found for the detected ecosystem,
the script exits successfully without doing anything.
The dnf prefix is shared by dnf, microdnf, and yum,
as they all operate on the same package ecosystem.

interactive.description:
Run in interactive mode.
By default, the installation runs non-interactively.
Set this flag to allow interactive prompts.

keep_repos.description:
Keep added repositories after installation.
By default, any repository drop-in files written during installation
are removed after the script finishes.
Set this flag to keep them permanently.

logfile.description: Log all output (stdout + stderr) to this file in addition to console.

no_clean.description:
Do not clean the package manager cache after installation.
By default, the package manager's cache is cleaned after installation.
This option skips that step.

no_update.description:
Do not update package lists before installation.
By default, package lists are refreshed before installing packages.
This option skips that step.
